revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '[[ファイル:滝尾百穴01.jpg|thumb|right|300px|滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] '''滝尾百穴横穴古墳群'''（たきおひゃっけつよこあなこ...'
revision.text:
[[ファイル:滝尾百穴01.jpg|thumb|right|300px|滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] '''滝尾百穴横穴古墳群'''（たきおひゃっけつよこあなこふんぐん）は、[[大分県]][[大分市]]羽田にある[[古墳時代]]後期後半の[[横穴墓]]。'''滝尾百穴'''とも呼ばれる。大分市の[[史跡]]に指定されている。
== 概要 == [[大分市立滝尾中学校]]のグラウンドの北側にある火山層の[[崖]]に、様々な大きさの75の横穴墓が3-4段に並んでいる。横穴墓の蓋部は崩壊して現存していない。横穴墓には多くのバリエーションがある。平面形状は正方形のもの、長方形のものがあり、天井の形状もドーム状、かまぼこ状、鴨居のような意匠のある妻入り家形、[[寄棟造|寄せ棟]]形等がある。また、床面に棺台があるものや、羨道の片方に袖石を有するものもある<ref>[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/www/contents/1094724531265/index.html 大分市の歴史探訪3（中部地区の文化財）] 大分市</ref><ref name="visit-oita">[http://www.visit-oita.jp/spot/c_201-0003.html 滝尾百穴横穴古墳群 | スポット情報] 日本一の「おんせん県」大分県の観光情報公式サイト（公益社団法人ツーリズムおおいた）</ref><ref>[http://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 滝尾百穴横穴古墳群｜観光スポット] 一般社団法人 大分市観光協会</ref>。
本横穴古墳群は、集団墓的な性質を持つ家族墓であると考えられている。
== 交通 == * バス - [[大分バス]] 萩原・[[あけのアクロスタウン|アクロス]]前・[[パークプレイス大分|パークプレイス]]・パークシティ明野・南下郡行きで米良バイパス入口下車。徒歩約10分<ref name="visit-oita" />。
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.visit-oita.jp/spot/c_201-0003.html 滝尾百穴横穴古墳群 | スポット情報] 日本一の「おんせん県」大分県の観光情報公式サイト（公益社団法人ツーリズムおおいた） * [http://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 滝尾百穴横穴古墳群｜観光スポット] 一般社団法人 大分市観光協会
{{DEFAULTSORT:たきおひやつけつよこあなこふんくん}} [[Category:大分市の歴史]] [[Category:大分県の古墳]] [[Category:区市町村指定史跡]]

横穴古墳群|lat_deg=33|lat_min=12|lat_sec=57.8|lon_deg=131|lon_min=38|lon_sec=01.7|mark=横穴墓.png|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=大分県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|12|57.8|N|131|38|01.7|E}}}} }} '''滝尾百穴横穴古墳群'''（たきおひゃっけつよこあなこふんぐん）は、[[大分県]][[大分市]]羽田にある[[古墳時代]]後期後半の[[横穴墓]]群。'''滝尾百穴'''とも呼ばれる。大分市の[[史跡]]に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[大分市立滝尾中学校]]のグラウンドの北側にある火山灰層の[[崖]]に、様々な大きさの75基の横穴墓が3-4段に並んでいる。横穴墓の蓋部は崩壊して現存していない。横穴墓には多くのバリエーションがある。平面形状は正方形のもの、長方形のものがあり、天井の形状もドーム状、かまぼこ状、鴨居のような意匠のある妻入り家形、[[寄棟造|寄せ棟]]形等がある。また、床面に棺台があるものや、羨道の片方に袖石を有するものもある[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html 大分市の歴史探訪3（中部地区の文化財）] 大分市[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 滝尾百穴横穴古墳群 | スポット情報] 日本一の「おんせん県」大分県の観光情報公式サイト（公益社団法人ツーリズムおおいた）[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 滝尾百穴横穴古墳群｜観光スポット] 一般社団法人 大分市観光協会。 本横穴古墳群は、集団墓的な性質を持つ家族墓であると考えられている。 == 交通 == * バス - [[大分バス]] 萩原・[[あけのアクロスタウン|アクロス]]前・[[パークプレイス大分|パークプレイス]]・パークシティ明野・南下郡行きで米良バイパス入口下車。徒歩約10分。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 滝尾百穴横穴古墳群 | スポット情報] 日本一の「おんせん県」大分県の観光情報公式サイト（公益社団法人ツーリズムおおいた） * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 滝尾百穴横穴古墳群｜観光スポット] 一般社団法人 大分市観光協会 {{DEFAULTSORT:たきおひやつけつよこあなこふんくん}} [[Category:横穴墓]] [[Category:群集墳]] [[Category:大分市の歴史]] [[Category:大分県の古墳]] [[Category:大分県内の市町村指定史跡]]

revision.comment: Created page with " == Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]. === Tōhoku Region === * {{ill|Yamahata Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|山畑横穴群}} ({{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|ja|大崎市}}, [[Miyagi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site..."
revision.text:
== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]].
=== Tōhoku Region === * {{ill|Yamahata Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|山畑横穴群}} ({{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|ja|大崎市}}, [[Miyagi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 26 tombs * {{ill|Atagoyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|愛宕山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台市}}) * {{ill|Mogasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|茂ヶ崎横穴墓群}} (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) - 25 tombs, no longer extant * {{ill|Sōzenji Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|宗禅寺横穴墓群}} (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) * {{ill|Kiyotosaku Yokoguchi|ja|清戸迫横穴墓}} ({{ill|Futaba, Fukushima|ja|双葉町}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Izumizaki Yokoguchi|ja|泉崎横穴墓}} ({{ill|Nishishirakawa District|ja|西白河郡}}, {{ill|Izumizaki, Fukushima|ja|泉崎村}}) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nakada Yokoguchi|ja|中田横穴}} ({{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Hayama Yokoguchi|ja|羽山横穴}} ({{ill|Minamisōma|ja|南相馬市}}, Fukushima Prefecture) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Wada Daibutsu and Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|和田大仏及び横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Sukagawa|ja|須賀川市}}, Fukushima Prefecture) - Municipal Historic Site
=== Kantō Region === * {{ill|Jūgorō-ana Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|十五郎穴横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|ja|ひたちなか市}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) - Over 300 tombs * {{ill|Karagoshō Yokoguchi|ja|唐御所横穴}} ({{ill|Nasukaragawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, [[Tochigi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, part of the Hokōda and Wami Yokoguchi Clusters * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakka|ja|長岡百穴古墳}} ({{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture) - 52 tombs * {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakka|ja|吉見百穴}} ({{ill|Yoshimi, Saitama|ja|吉見町}}, [[Saitama Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Kuroiwa Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|黒岩横穴墓群}} (Yoshimi, Saitama) - Over 30 tombs * {{ill|Jūrō Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|十郎横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Hatoyama, Saitama|ja|鳩山町}}, Saitama) - 3 tombs * {{ill|Takinojō Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|滝之城横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Tokorozawa|ja|所沢市}}, Saitama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|北秋津横穴墓群}} (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|比丘尼山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|高根横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|長柄横穴群}} ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|ja|長柄町}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|出山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|ja|三鷹市}}, [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|谷沢川横穴群}} ({{ill|Setagaya|ja|世田谷区}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|荏子田横穴}} ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|ja|青葉区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|ja|市ヶ尾横穴古墳群}} (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Kanazawa River Basin Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|松ヶ崎横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichishiyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaoka Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|柏谷横穴群}} ({{ill|Kannami|ja|函南町}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|北江間横穴群}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|ja|伊豆の国市}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|江浦横穴群}} ({{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Yokoguchi Kofun|ja|法皇山横穴古墳}} ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|加賀市}}, [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|高井田横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|安福寺横穴群}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|十王免横穴群}} ({{ill|Matsue|ja|松江市}}, [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|狐谷横穴墓群}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|上塩冶横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|ja|出雲市}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|高津久横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|ja|知夫村}}, [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|ja|竹並遺跡}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|ja|行橋市}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|城山横穴群}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|ja|福智町}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|ja|水町遺跡}} ({{ill|Nōgata|ja|直方市}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|ja|古月横穴}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|ja|鞍手町}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|石貫ナギノ横穴群}} ({{ill|Tamana|ja|玉名市}}, [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|石貫穴観音横穴群}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|鍋田横穴群}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|ja|山鹿市}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|大村横穴群}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|ja|人吉市}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|四日市横穴群}} ({{ill|Usa|ja|宇佐市}}, [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|ja|滝尾百穴横穴古墳群}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|ja|大分市}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasunogaike Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|蓮ヶ池横穴群}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|ja|宮崎市}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Yokohama Tombs.
*

== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Toional Historic Site, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasunogaike Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Yokohama Tombs.
*

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Kashio River Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * {{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31 (Kashio River Cave Tomb Cluster)]'' (March 20, 2015).
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}} * {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} ({{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}) * {{ill|Kashio River Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} * {{ill|List of Yokohama City Designated and Registered Cultural Properties|ja|横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧}}
== External Links == *{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://teishoin.net/blog/003984.html|title=Current State of the Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|website=Kameno's Digital Photo Log|date=2010-09-22|accessdate=2020-07-22}}

{{Japanese Kofun |name=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |alias=Takiō Hyakketsu |image=[[File:Takiō Hyakketsu 01.jpg|300px]] |image_caption=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |location={{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda |coordinates={{Coord|33|12|57.8|N|131|38|01.7|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |construction_period=6th century (Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}) |historic_site_designation=Designated as a city historic site on January 9, 1974 (Shōwa 49){{Cite web|language=ja|author=Oita City Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/culturalproperty.html|title=Oita City's Cultural Properties|publisher=Oita City|date=2021-08-05|accessdate=2021-08-11}} |scale=75 cave tombs |map2={{Location map|Japan Oita|label=Takiō Hyakketsu
Cave Tomb Cluster|mark=Cave Tomb.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Oita Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster''' (滝尾百穴横穴古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|cave tombs|ja|横穴墓}} dating to the late 6th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located in {{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda. It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of forms, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造}}. Some tombs feature coffin platforms on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Cultural Properties of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * **By Bus** – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association [[Category:Cave tombs]] [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:History of Oita City]] [[Category:Kofun in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites of Oita Prefecture]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] == Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Toional Historic Site, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasunogaike Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). The site has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校}}. {{Japanese Kofun |name=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |alias=Takiō Hyakketsu |image=[[File:Takiō Hyakketsu 01.jpg|300px]] |image_caption=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |location={{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda |coordinates={{Coord|33|12|57.8|N|131|38|01.7|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |construction_period=6th century (Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}) |historic_site_designation=Designated as a city historic site on January 9, 1974 (Shōwa 49){{Cite web|language=ja|author=Oita City Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/culturalproperty.html|title=Oita City's Cultural Properties|publisher=Oita City|date=2021-08-05|accessdate=2021-08-11}} |scale=75 cave tombs |map2={{Location map|Japan Oita|label=Takiō Hyakketsu
Cave Tomb Cluster|mark=Cave Tomb.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Oita Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster''' (滝尾百穴横穴古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|cave tombs|ja|横穴墓}} dating to the late 6th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located in {{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda. It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of forms, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造}}. Some tombs feature coffin platforms on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Cultural Properties of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * **By Bus** – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association [[Category:Cave tombs]] [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:History of Oita City]] [[Category:Kofun in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites of Oita Prefecture]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] == Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Toional Historic Site, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasunogaike Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Yokohama Tombs. * [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Japanese burial practices]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[ja:横穴墓]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:kk]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]]

Cave Tomb Cluster|mark=Cave Tomb.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Oita Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster''' (滝尾百穴横穴古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|cave tombs|ja|横穴墓}} dating to the late 6th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located in {{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda. It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of forms, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造}}. Some tombs feature coffin platforms on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Cultural Properties of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association [[Category:Cave tombs]] [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:History of Oita City]] [[Category:Kofun in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites of Oita Prefecture]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] == Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Toional Historic Site, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). The tombs exhibit a variety of forms, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造}}. Some tombs feature coffin platforms on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Cultural Properties of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. The site has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of forms, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造}}. Some tombs feature coffin platforms on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Cultural Properties of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. {{Japanese Kofun |name=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |alias=Takiō Hyakketsu |image=[[File:Takiō Hyakketsu 01.jpg|300px]] |image_caption=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |location={{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda |coordinates={{Coord|33|12|57.8|N|131|38|01.7|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |construction_period=6th century (Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}) |historic_site_designation=Designated as a city historic site on January 9, 1974 (Shōwa 49){{Cite web|language=ja|author=Oita City Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/culturalproperty.html|title=Oita City's Cultural Properties|publisher=Oita City|date=2021-08-05|accessdate=2021-08-11}} |scale=75 cave tombs |map2={{Location map|Japan Oita|label=Takiō Hyakketsu
Cave Tomb Cluster|mark=Cave Tomb.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Oita Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster''' (滝尾百穴横穴古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|cave tombs|ja|横穴墓}} dating to the late 6th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located in {{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda. It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of forms, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造}}. Some tombs feature coffin platforms on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Cultural Properties of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association [[Category:Cave tombs]] [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:History of Oita City]] [[Category:Kofun in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites of Oita Prefecture]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] == Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Toional Historic Site, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of forms, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造}}. Some tombs feature coffin platforms on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Cultural Properties of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association [[Category:Cave tombs]] [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:History of Oita City]] [[Category:Kofun in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites of Oita Prefecture]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] == Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Toional Historic Site, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of forms, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造}}. Some tombs feature coffin platforms on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Cultural Properties of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. revision.comment: Text replacement - "羨道}}" to "羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}"
revision.text:
{{Japanese Kofun |name=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |alias=Takiō Hyakketsu |image=[[File:Takiō Hyakketsu 01.jpg|300px]] |image_caption=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |location={{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda |coordinates={{Coord|33|12|57.8|N|131|38|01.7|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |construction_period=6th century (Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}) |historic_site_designation=Designated as a city historic site on January 9, 1974 (Shōwa 49){{Cite web|language=ja|author=Oita City Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/culturalproperty.html|title=Oita City's Cultural Properties|publisher=Oita City|date=2021-08-05|accessdate=2021-08-11}} |scale=75 cave tombs |map2={{Location map|Japan Oita|label=Takiō Hyakketsu
Cave Tomb Cluster|mark=Cave Tomb.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Oita Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster''' (滝尾百穴横穴古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|cave tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} dating to the late 6th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located in {{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda. It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of forms, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造}}. Some tombs feature coffin platforms on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Cultural Properties of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association [[Category:Cave tombs]] [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:History of Oita City]] [[Category:Kofun in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites of Oita Prefecture]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] == Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Toional Historic Site, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Yokohama Tombs. * [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Japanese burial practices]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] {{Japanese Kofun |name=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |alias=Takiō Hyakketsu |image=[[File:Takiō Hyakketsu 01.jpg|300px]] |image_caption=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |location={{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda |coordinates={{Coord|33|12|57.8|N|131|38|01.7|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |construction_period=6th century (Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}) |historic_site_designation=Designated as a city historic site on January 9, 1974 (Shōwa 49){{Cite web|language=ja|author=Oita City Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/culturalproperty.html|title=Oita City's Cultural Properties|publisher=Oita City|date=2021-08-05|accessdate=2021-08-11}} |scale=75 cave tombs |image=滝尾百穴01.jpg |map2={{Location map|Japan Oita|label=Takiō Hyakketsu
Cave Tomb Cluster|mark=Cave Tomb.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Oita Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster''' (滝尾百穴横穴古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|cave tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} dating to the late 6th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located in {{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda. It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of forms, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造}}. Some tombs feature coffin platforms on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Cultural Properties of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association [[Category:Cave tombs]] [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:History of Oita City]] [[Category:Kofun in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites of Oita Prefecture]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] == Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Toional Historic Site, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Cave Tombs. * [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Japanese burial practices]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] {{Japanese Kofun |name=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |alias=Takiō Hyakketsu |image=[[File:Takiō Hyakketsu 01.jpg|300px]] |image_caption=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |location={{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda |coordinates={{Coord|33|12|57.8|N|131|38|01.7|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |construction_period=6th century (Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}) |historic_site_designation=Designated as a city historic site on January 9, 1974 (Shōwa 49){{Cite web|language=ja|author=Oita City Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/culturalproperty.html|title=Oita City's Cultural Properties|publisher=Oita City|date=2021-08-05|accessdate=2021-08-11}} |scale=75 cave tombs |image=滝尾百穴01.jpg |map2={{Location map|Japan Oita|label=Takiō Hyakketsu
Cave Tomb Cluster|mark=Cave Tomb.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Oita Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster''' (滝尾百穴横穴古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|cave tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} dating to the late 6th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located in {{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda. It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of forms, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造}}. Some tombs feature coffin platforms on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Cultural Properties of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association [[Category:Cave tombs]] [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:History of Oita City]] [[Category:Kofun in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites of Oita Prefecture]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Toional Historic Site, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). {{Japanese Kofun |name=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |alias=Takiō Hyakketsu |image=[[File:Takiō Hyakketsu 01.jpg|300px]] |image_caption=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |location={{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda |coordinates={{Coord|33|12|57.8|N|131|38|01.7|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |construction_period=6th century (Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}) |historic_site_designation=Designated as a city historic site on January 9, 1974 (Shōwa 49){{Cite web|language=ja|author=Oita City Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/culturalproperty.html|title=Oita City's Cultural Properties|publisher=Oita City|date=2021-08-05|accessdate=2021-08-11}} |scale=75 cave tombs |image=滝尾百穴01.jpg |map2={{Location map|Japan Oita|label=Takiō Hyakketsu
Cave Tomb Cluster|mark=Cave Tomb.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Oita Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster''' (滝尾百穴横穴古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|cave tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} dating to the late 6th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located in {{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda. It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of forms, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造}}. Some tombs feature coffin platforms on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Cultural Properties of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association [[Category:Cave tombs]] [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:History of Oita City]] [[Category:Kofun in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites of Oita Prefecture]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Toional Historic Site, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Cave Tombs. * [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Japanese burial practices]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] Cave Tomb Cluster|mark=Cave Tomb.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Oita Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster''' (滝尾百穴横穴古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|cave tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} dating to the late 6th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located in {{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda. It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of forms, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造}}. Some tombs feature coffin platforms on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Cultural Properties of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association [[Category:Cave tombs]] [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:History of Oita City]] [[Category:Kofun in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites of Oita Prefecture]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Toional Historic Site, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). Some tombs feature coffin platforms on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Cultural Properties of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. {{Japanese Kofun |name=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |alias=Takiō Hyakketsu |image=[[File:Takiō Hyakketsu 01.jpg|300px]] |image_caption=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |location={{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda |coordinates={{Coord|33|12|57.8|N|131|38|01.7|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |construction_period=6th century (Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}) |historic_site_designation=Designated as a city historic site on January 9, 1974 (Shōwa 49){{Cite web|language=ja|author=Oita City Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/culturalproperty.html|title=Oita City's Cultural Properties|publisher=Oita City|date=2021-08-05|accessdate=2021-08-11}} |scale=75 cave tombs |image=滝尾百穴01.jpg |map2={{Location map|Japan Oita|label=Takiō Hyakketsu
Cave Tomb Cluster|mark=Cave Tomb.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Oita Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster''' (滝尾百穴横穴古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|cave tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} dating to the late 6th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located in {{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda. It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of forms, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造}}. Some tombs feature coffin platforms on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Cultural Properties of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Cave Tomb Cluster|mark=Cave Tomb.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Oita Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster''' (滝尾百穴横穴古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|cave tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} dating to the late 6th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located in {{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda. It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of forms, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造}}. Some tombs feature coffin platforms on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Cultural Properties of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Some tombs feature coffin platforms on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Cultural Properties of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. {{Japanese Kofun |name=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |alias=Takiō Hyakketsu |image=[[File:Takiō Hyakketsu 01.jpg|300px]] |image_caption=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |location={{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda |coordinates={{Coord|33|12|57.8|N|131|38|01.7|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |construction_period=6th century (Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}) |historic_site_designation=Designated as a city historic site on January 9, 1974 (Shōwa 49){{Cite web|language=ja|author=Oita City Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/culturalproperty.html|title=Oita City's Cultural Properties|publisher=Oita City|date=2021-08-05|accessdate=2021-08-11}} |scale=75 cave tombs |image=滝尾百穴01.jpg |map2={{Location map|Japan Oita|label=Takiō Hyakketsu
Cave Tomb Cluster|mark=Cave Tomb.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Oita Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster''' (滝尾百穴横穴古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|cave tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} dating to the late 6th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located in {{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda. It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of forms, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造}}. Some tombs feature coffin platforms on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Cultural Properties of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association [[Category:Cave tombs]] [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:History of Oita City]] [[Category:Kofun in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites of Oita Prefecture]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Toional Historic Site, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). The site has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of forms, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造}}. Some tombs feature coffin platforms on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Cultural Properties of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association [[Category:Cave tombs]] [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:History of Oita City]] [[Category:Kofun in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites of Oita Prefecture]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Toional Historic Site, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Cave Tombs. * [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Japanese burial practices]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[ja:横穴墓]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]][[sn:Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]][[sn:Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]][[sn:Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]][[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[sn:Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association [[Category:Cave tombs]] [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:History of Oita City]] [[Category:Kofun in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites of Oita Prefecture]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Toional Historic Site, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). revision.comment: doing more mass replacements to fix the issues
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |alias=Takiō Hyakketsu |image=[[File:Takiō Hyakketsu 01.jpg|300px]] |image_caption=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |location={{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda |coordinates={{Coord|33|12|57.8|N|131|38|01.7|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |construction_period=6th century (Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}) |historic_site_designation=Designated as a city historic site on January 9, 1974 (Shōwa 49){{Cite web|language=ja|author=Oita City Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/culturalproperty.html|title=Oita City's Cultural Properties|publisher=Oita City|date=2021-08-05|accessdate=2021-08-11}} |scale=75 cave tombs |image=滝尾百穴01.jpg |map={{Location map|Japan Oita|label=Takiō Hyakketsu
Cave Tomb Cluster|mark=Cave Tomb.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Oita Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster''' (滝尾百穴横穴古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|cave tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} dating to the late 6th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located in {{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda. It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of forms, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造}}. Some tombs feature coffin platforms on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Cultural Properties of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association [[Category:Cave tombs]] [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:History of Oita City]] [[Category:Kofun in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites of Oita Prefecture]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Toional Historic Site, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013).
