Cave Tomb Cluster|mark=Cave Tomb.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Oita Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster''' (滝尾百穴横穴古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|cave tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} dating to the late 6th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located in {{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda. It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of forms, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings typed in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造}}. Some tombs feature coffin platforms on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Cultural Properties of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association [[Category:Cave tombs]] [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:History of Oita City]] [[Category:Kofun in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites of Oita Prefecture]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Toional Historic Site, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). revision.comment: trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |alternate_name=Takiō Hyakketsu |image=[[File:Takiō Hyakketsu 01.jpg|300px]] |image_caption=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |location={{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda |coordinates={{Coord|33|12|57.8|N|131|38|01.7|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |built=6th century (Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}) |historic_site_designation=Designated as a city historic site on January 9, 1974 (Shōwa 49){{Cite web|language=ja|author=Oita City Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/culturalproperty.html|title=Oita City's other_designation|publisher=Oita City|date=2021-08-05|accessdate=2021-08-11}} |map_size=75 cave tombs |image=滝尾百穴01.jpg |map={{Location map|Japan Oita|label=Takiō Hyakketsu
Cave Tomb Cluster|mark=Cave Tomb.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Oita Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster''' (滝尾百穴横穴古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|cave tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} dating to the late 6th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located in {{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda. It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of forms, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造}}. Some tombs feature coffin platforms on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (other_designation of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association [[Category:Cave tombs]] [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:History of Oita City]] [[Category:Kofun in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites of Oita Prefecture]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Toional Historic Site, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Cave Tombs. * [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Japanese burial practices]] [[Category:kk]] Some tombs feature coffin platforms on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (other_designation of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Cave Tomb Cluster|mark=Cave Tomb.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Oita Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster''' (滝尾百穴横穴古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|cave tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} dating to the late 6th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located in {{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda. It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of forms, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造}}. Some tombs feature coffin platforms on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (other_designation of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Some tombs feature coffin platforms on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (other_designation of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Cave Tomb Cluster|mark=Cave Tomb.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Oita Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster''' (滝尾百穴横穴古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|cave tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} dating to the late 6th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located in {{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda. It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been designated as a {{ill|other_designation|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of forms, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造}}. Some tombs feature coffin platforms on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (other_designation of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Some tombs feature coffin platforms on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (other_designation of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Information] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association [[Category:Cave tombs]] [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:History of Oita City]] [[Category:Kofun in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal other_designations of Oita Prefecture]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Toional other_designation, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal other_designation, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National other_designation == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |alternate_name=Takiō Hyakketsu |image=[[File:Takiō Hyakketsu 01.jpg|300px]] |image_caption=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |location={{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda |coordinates={{Coord|33|12|57.8|N|131|38|01.7|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |built=6th century (Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}) |historic_site_other_designation=Designated as a city historic site on January 9, 1974 (Shōwa 49){{Cite web|language=ja|author=Oita City Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/culturalproperty.html|title=Oita City's other_other_designation|publisher=Oita City|date=2021-08-05|accessdate=2021-08-11}} |map_size=75 cave tombs |image=滝尾百穴01.jpg |map={{location map|Japan Oita|map_dot_label=Takiō Hyakketsu
Cave Tomb Cluster|mark=Cave Tomb.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Oita Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster''' (滝尾百穴横穴古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|cave tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} dating to the late 6th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located in {{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda. It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been other_designation1 as a {{ill|other_other_designation|ja|史跡|1=other_other_designation|lt=other_other_designation of Japan}}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of types, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造}}. Some tombs feature coffin plattypes on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (other_other_designation of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association [[Category:Cave tombs]] [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:History of Oita City]] [[Category:Kofun in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal other_other_designations of Oita Prefecture]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Toional other_other_designation, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal other_other_designation, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National other_other_designation == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). The site has been other_designation1 as a {{ill|other_other_designation|ja|史跡|1=other_other_designation|lt=other_other_designation of Japan}}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of types, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造}}. Some tombs feature coffin plattypes on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (other_other_designation of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association [[Category:Cave tombs]] [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:History of Oita City]] [[Category:Kofun in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal other_other_designations of Oita Prefecture]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Toional other_other_designation, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal other_other_designation, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National other_other_designation == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Cave Tomb Cluster|mark=Cave Tomb.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Oita Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster''' (滝尾百穴横穴古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|cave tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} dating to the late 6th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located in {{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda. It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been other_designation1 as a {{ill|other_other_designation|ja|史跡|1=other_other_designation|lt=other_other_designation of Japan}}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of types, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造}}. Some tombs feature coffin plattypes on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (other_other_designation of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Cave Tomb Cluster|mark=Cave Tomb.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Oita Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster''' (滝尾百穴横穴古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|cave tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} dating to the late 6th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located in {{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda. It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been other_designation1 as a {{ill|other_designation|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of types, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造}}. Some tombs feature coffin plattypes on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (other_designation of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association [[Category:Cave tombs]] [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:History of Oita City]] [[Category:Kofun in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal other_designations of Oita Prefecture]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Toional other_designation, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal other_designation, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National other_designation == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |alternate_name=Takiō Hyakketsu |image=[[File:Takiō Hyakketsu 01.jpg|300px]] |image_caption=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |location={{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda |coordinates={{Coord|33|12|57.8|N|131|38|01.7|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |built=6th century (Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}) |historic_site_other_designation=Designated as a city historic site on January 9, 1974 (Shōwa 49){{Cite web|language=ja|author=Oita City Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/culturalproperty.html|title=Oita City's other_designation|publisher=Oita City|date=2021-08-05|accessdate=2021-08-11}} |map_size=75 cave tombs |image=滝尾百穴01.jpg |map={{location map|Japan Oita|map_dot_label=Takiō Hyakketsu
Cave Tomb Cluster|mark=Cave Tomb.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Oita Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster''' (滝尾百穴横穴古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|cave tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} dating to the late 6th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located in {{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda. It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been other_designation1 as a {{ill|other_designation|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of types, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造}}. Some tombs feature coffin plattypes on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (other_designation of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association [[Category:Cave tombs]] [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:History of Oita City]] [[Category:Kofun in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal other_designations of Oita Prefecture]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Toional other_designation, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal other_designation, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National other_designation == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |alternate_name=Takiō Hyakketsu |image=[[File:Takiō Hyakketsu 01.jpg|300px]] |image_caption=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |location={{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda |coordinates={{Coord|33|12|57.8|N|131|38|01.7|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |built=6th century (Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}) |historic_site_other_designation=Designated as a city historic site on January 9, 1974 (Shōwa 49){{Cite web|language=ja|author=Oita City Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/culturalproperty.html|title=Oita City's other_designation|publisher=Oita City|date=2021-08-05|accessdate=2021-08-11}} |map_size=75 cave tombs |image=滝尾百穴01.jpg |map={{location map|Japan Oita|map_dot_label=Takiō Hyakketsu
Cave Tomb Cluster|mark=Cave Tomb.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Oita Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster''' (滝尾百穴横穴古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|cave tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} dating to the late 6th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located in {{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda. It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been other_designation1 as a {{ill|other_designation|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of types, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造}}. Some tombs feature coffin plattypes on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (other_designation of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association [[Category:Cave tombs]] [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:History of Oita City]] [[Category:Kofun in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal other_designations of Oita Prefecture]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Toional other_designation, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal other_designation, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National other_designation == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Cave Tombs. * [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Japanese burial practices]] [[Category:kk]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |alternate_name=Takiō Hyakketsu |image=[[File:Takiō Hyakketsu 01.jpg|300px]] |image_caption=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |location={{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda |coordinates={{Coord|33|12|57.8|N|131|38|01.7|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |built=6th century (Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}) |historic_site_other_designation=Designated as a city historic site on January 9, 1974 (Shōwa 49){{Cite web|language=ja|author=Oita City Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/culturalproperty.html|title=Oita City's Historic site|publisher=Oita City|date=2021-08-05|accessdate=2021-08-11}} |map_size=75 cave tombs |image=滝尾百穴01.jpg |map={{location map|Japan Oita|map_dot_label=Takiō Hyakketsu
Cave Tomb Cluster|mark=Cave Tomb.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Oita Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster''' (滝尾百穴横穴古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|cave tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} dating to the late 6th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located in {{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda. It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been Historic site as a {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of types, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造}}. Some tombs feature coffin plattypes on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Historic site of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association [[Category:Cave tombs]] [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:History of Oita City]] [[Category:Kofun in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic sites of Oita Prefecture]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Toional Historic site, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic site == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Cave Tombs. * [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Japanese burial practices]] [[Category:kk]] Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |alternate_name=Takiō Hyakketsu |image=[[File:Takiō Hyakketsu 01.jpg|300px]] |image_caption=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |location={{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda |coordinates={{Coord|33|12|57.8|N|131|38|01.7|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |built=6th century (Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}) |historic_site_Historic Site=Designated as a city historic site on January 9, 1974 (Shōwa 49){{Cite web|language=ja|author=Oita City Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/culturalproperty.html|title=Oita City's Historic site|publisher=Oita City|date=2021-08-05|accessdate=2021-08-11}} |map_size=75 cave tombs |image=滝尾百穴01.jpg |map={{location map|Japan Oita|map_dot_label=Takiō Hyakketsu
Cave Tomb Cluster|mark=Cave Tomb.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Oita Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster''' (滝尾百穴横穴古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|cave tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} dating to the late 6th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located in {{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda. It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been Historic site as a {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of types, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造}}. Some tombs feature coffin plattypes on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Historic site of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association [[Category:Cave tombs]] [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:History of Oita City]] [[Category:Kofun in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic sites of Oita Prefecture]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Toional Historic site, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic site == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association [[Category:Cave tombs]] [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:History of Oita City]] [[Category:Kofun in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic sites of Oita Prefecture]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Toional Historic site, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic site == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |alternate_name=Takiō Hyakketsu |image=[[File:Takiō Hyakketsu 01.jpg|300px]] |image_caption=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |location={{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda |coordinates={{Coord|33|12|57.8|N|131|38|01.7|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |built=6th century (Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}) |historic_site_Historic Site=Designated as a city historic site on January 9, 1974 (Shōwa 49){{Cite web|language=ja|author=Oita City Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/culturalproperty.html|title=Oita City's Historic site|publisher=Oita City|date=2021-08-05|accessdate=2021-08-11}} |map_size=75 cave tombs |image=滝尾百穴01.jpg |map={{location map|Japan Oita|map_dot_label=Takiō Hyakketsu
Cave Tomb Cluster|mark=Cave Tomb.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Oita Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster''' (滝尾百穴横穴古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|cave tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} dating to the late 6th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, located in {{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Haneda. It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been Historic site as a {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of types, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造}}. Some tombs feature coffin plattypes on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Historic site of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Toional Historic site, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic site == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |alternate_name=Takiō Hyakketsu |image=[[File:Takiō Hyakketsu 01.jpg|300px]] |image_caption=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |location={{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県|12=draft}}, Haneda |coordinates={{Coord|33|12|57.8|N|131|38|01.7|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |built=6th century (Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}) |historic_site_Historic Site=Designated as a city historic site on January 9, 1974 (Shōwa 49){{Cite web|language=ja|author=Oita City Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/culturalproperty.html|title=Oita City's Historic site|publisher=Oita City|date=2021-08-05|accessdate=2021-08-11}} |map_size=75 cave tombs |image=滝尾百穴01.jpg |map={{location map|Japan Oita|map_dot_label=Takiō Hyakketsu
Cave Tomb Cluster|mark=Cave Tomb.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Oita Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster''' (滝尾百穴横穴古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|cave tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}} dating to the late 6th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県|12=draft}}, Haneda. It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been Historic site as a {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校|12=draft}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of types, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造|12=draft}}. Some tombs feature coffin plattypes on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=draft}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Historic site of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス|12=draft}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン|12=draft}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分|12=draft}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Toional Historic site, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|12=draft}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|12=draft}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic site == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Cave Tombs. * [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Cave tombs]] [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:History of Oita City]] [[Category:Kofun in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic sites of Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Japanese burial practices]] [[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] }} [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] [[ja:大分市]] [[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] [[ja:宮崎市]] [[ja:横穴墓]] [[sn:Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:akio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]]

It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |alternate_name=Takiō Hyakketsu |image=[[File:Takiō Hyakketsu 01.jpg|300px]] |image_caption=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |location={{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県|12=draft}}, Haneda |coordinates={{Coord|33|12|57.8|N|131|38|01.7|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |built=6th century (Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}) |historic_site_Historic Site=Designated as a city historic site on January 9, 1974 (Shōwa 49){{Cite web|language=ja|author=Oita City Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/culturalproperty.html|title=Oita City's Historic site|publisher=Oita City|date=2021-08-05|accessdate=2021-08-11}} |map_size=75 cave tombs |image=滝尾百穴01.jpg |map={{location map|Japan Oita|map_dot_label=Takiō Hyakketsu
Cave Tomb Cluster|mark=Cave Tomb.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Oita Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster''' (滝尾百穴横穴古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|cave tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}} dating to the late 6th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県|12=draft}}, Haneda. It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been Historic site as a {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校|12=draft}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of types, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造|12=draft}}. Some tombs feature coffin plattypes on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=draft}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Historic site of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス|12=draft}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン|12=draft}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分|12=draft}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Toional Historic site, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|12=draft}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|12=draft}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic site == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |alternate_name=Takiō Hyakketsu |image=[[File:Takiō Hyakketsu 01.jpg|300px]] |image_caption=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |location={{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県|12=draft}}, Haneda |coordinates={{Coord|33|12|57.8|N|131|38|01.7|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |built=6th century (Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}) |historic_site_Historic Site=Designated as a city historic site on January 9, 1974 (Shōwa 49){{Cite web|language=ja|author=Oita City Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/culturalproperty.html|title=Oita City's Historic site|publisher=Oita City|date=2021-08-05|accessdate=2021-08-11}} |map_size=75 cave tombs |image=滝尾百穴01.jpg |map={{location map|Japan Oita|map_dot_label=Takiō Hyakketsu
Cave Tomb Cluster|mark=Cave Tomb.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Oita Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster''' (滝尾百穴横穴古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|cave tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}} dating to the late 6th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県|12=draft}}, Haneda. It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been Historic site as a {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校|12=draft}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of types, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造|12=draft}}. Some tombs feature coffin plattypes on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=draft}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Historic site of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス|12=draft}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン|12=draft}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分|12=draft}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Toional Historic site, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|12=draft}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|12=draft}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic site == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Cave Tomb Cluster|mark=Cave Tomb.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Oita Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster''' (滝尾百穴横穴古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|cave tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}} dating to the late 6th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Oita City|ja|大分市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Oita Prefecture|ja|大分県|12=draft}}, Haneda. It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been Historic site as a {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校|12=draft}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of types, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Yosemune-zukuri|ja|寄棟造|12=draft}}. Some tombs feature coffin plattypes on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=draft}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Historic site of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス|12=draft}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン|12=draft}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分|12=draft}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Toional Historic site, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|12=draft}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|12=draft}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic site == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999).
