Cave Tomb Cluster|mark=Cave Tomb.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Oita Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster''' (滝尾百穴横穴古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|cave tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cave tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} dating to the late 6th century during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], located in [[:en:Ōita (city)|Oita City]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Oita Prefecture]], Haneda. It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been Historic site as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oita City Takiō Junior High School|1=Oita City Takiō Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432734}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of types, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as [[:en:Hip roof|Yosemune-zukuri]]. Some tombs feature coffin plattypes on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/genkan|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Historic site of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oita Bus|1=Oita Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432629}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akros|1=AKENO ACROSS TOWN|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257072}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Park Place Oita|1=Park Place Oita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11329797}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Toional Historic site, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita, Ōita|qq=}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki, Miyazaki|qq=}}, [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic site == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). Cave Tomb Cluster|mark=Cave Tomb.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Oita Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster''' (滝尾百穴横穴古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|cave tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cave tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} dating to the late 6th century during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], located in [[:en:Ōita (city)|Oita City]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Oita Prefecture]], Haneda. It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been Historic site as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oita City Takiō Junior High School|1=Oita City Takiō Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432734}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of types, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as [[:en:Hip roof|Yosemune-zukuri]]. Some tombs feature coffin plattypes on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/genkan|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Historic site of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oita Bus|1=Oita Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432629}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akros|1=AKENO ACROSS TOWN|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257072}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Park Place Oita|1=Park Place Oita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11329797}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Toional Historic site, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita, Ōita|qq=}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki, Miyazaki|qq=}}, [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic site == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Cave Tombs. * {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|滝尾百穴横穴古墳群|version=98185626|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] [[ja:大分市]] [[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] [[ja:宮崎市]] [[ja:横穴墓]] {{wikidata link|Q18338700}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Oita, Oita]] [[Category:Kofun of Oita prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |alternate_name=Takiō Hyakketsu |image=[[File:Takiō Hyakketsu 01.jpg|300px]] |image_caption=Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster |location={{ill|Ōita (city)|en|Ōita (city)|WD=Q199609|lt=Oita City}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Oita Prefecture}}, Haneda |coordinates={{Coord|33|12|57.8|N|131|38|01.7|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |built=6th century (Late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}) |historic_site_Historic Site=Designated as a city historic site on January 9, 1974 (Shōwa 49){{Cite web|language=ja|author=Oita City Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/culturalproperty.html|title=Oita City's Historic site|publisher=Oita City|date=2021-08-05|accessdate=2021-08-11}} |map_size=75 cave tombs |image=滝尾百穴01.jpg |map={{location map|Japan Oita|map_dot_label=Takiō Hyakketsu
Cave Tomb Cluster|mark=Cave Tomb.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Oita Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster''' (滝尾百穴横穴古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|cave tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cave tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} dating to the late 6th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, located in {{ill|Ōita (city)|en|Ōita (city)|WD=Q199609|lt=Oita City}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Oita Prefecture}}, Haneda. It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been Historic site as a {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oita City Takiō Junior High School|1=Oita City Takiō Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432734}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of types, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Hip roof|en|Hip roof|WD=Q162034|lt=Yosemune-zukuri}}. Some tombs feature coffin plattypes on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/genkan|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Historic site of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oita Bus|1=Oita Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432629}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akros|1=AKENO ACROSS TOWN|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257072}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Park Place Oita|1=Park Place Oita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11329797}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Toional Historic site, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita, Ōita|qq=}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki, Miyazaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture}}) - National Historic site == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Some tombs feature coffin plattypes on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/genkan|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Historic site of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oita Bus|1=Oita Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432629}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akros|1=AKENO ACROSS TOWN|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257072}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Park Place Oita|1=Park Place Oita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11329797}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Cave Tomb Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Toional Historic site, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita, Ōita|qq=}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki, Miyazaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture}}) - National Historic site == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Cave Tombs. * {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|滝尾百穴横穴古墳群|version=98185626|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] [[ja:大分市]] [[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] [[ja:宮崎市]] [[ja:横穴墓]] {{wikidata link|Q18338700}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] [[Category:History of Oita, Oita]] [[Category:Kofun of Oita prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Oita|WD=Q199609}}|coordinates={{Coord|33.216055555555556|N|131.63380555555557|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Japanese Kofun |name=Takiō Hyakketsu Yokoanabo Cluster |alternate_name=Takiō Hyakketsu |image=[[File:Takiō Hyakketsu 01.jpg|300px]] |image_caption=Takiō Hyakketsu Yokoanabo Cluster |location={{ill|Ōita (city)|en|Ōita (city)|WD=Q199609|lt=Oita City}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Oita Prefecture}}, Haneda |coordinates={{Coord|33|12|57.8|N|131|38|01.7|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |built=6th century (Late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}) |historic_site_Historic Site=Designated as a city historic site on January 9, 1974 (Shōwa 49){{Cite web|language=ja|author=Oita City Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/culturalproperty.html|title=Oita City's Historic site|publisher=Oita City|date=2021-08-05|accessdate=2021-08-11}} |map_size=75 cave tombs |image=滝尾百穴01.jpg |map={{location map|Japan Oita|map_dot_label=Takiō Hyakketsu
Yokoanabo Cluster|mark=Yokoanabo.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Oita Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takiō Hyakketsu Yokoanabo Cluster''' (滝尾百穴横穴古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|cave tombs|lt=Yokoanabos|ja|横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cave tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}} dating to the late 6th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, located in {{ill|Ōita (city)|en|Ōita (city)|WD=Q199609|lt=Oita City}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Oita Prefecture}}, Haneda. It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been Historic site as a {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oita City Takiō Junior High School|1=Oita City Takiō Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432734}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of types, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Hip roof|en|Hip roof|WD=Q162034|lt=Yosemune-zukuri}}. Some tombs feature coffin plattypes on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/genkan|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Historic site of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Yokoanabo Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Yokoanabo Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oita Bus|1=Oita Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432629}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akros|1=AKENO ACROSS TOWN|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257072}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Park Place Oita|1=Park Place Oita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11329797}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Yokoanabo Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Yokoanabo Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association == Distribution and Notable Yokoanabo Toional Historic site, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoanabo Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takio Hyakka Yokoanabo Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita, Ōita|qq=}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki, Miyazaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture}}) - National Historic site == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Yokoanabos. * {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|滝尾百穴横穴古墳群|version=98185626|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] [[ja:大分市]] [[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] [[ja:宮崎市]] [[ja:横穴墓]] {{wikidata link|Q18338700}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] [[Category:History of Oita, Oita]] [[Category:Kofun of Oita prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:pages]]

A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of types, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Hip roof|en|Hip roof|WD=Q162034|lt=Yosemune-zukuri|1=Hip roof}}. Some tombs feature coffin plattypes on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/genkan|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Historic site of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Yokoanabo Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Yokoanabo Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oita Bus|1=Oita Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432629}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akros|1=AKENO ACROSS TOWN|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257072}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Park Place Oita|1=Park Place Oita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11329797}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Yokoanabo Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Yokoanabo Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association == Distribution and Notable Yokoanabo Toional Historic site, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoanabo Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takio Hyakka Yokoanabo Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita, Ōita|qq=}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki, Miyazaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}}) - National Historic site == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Yokoanabos. * {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|滝尾百穴横穴古墳群|version=98185626|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] [[ja:大分市]] [[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] [[ja:宮崎市]] [[ja:横穴墓]] {{wikidata link|Q18338700}} [[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] [[Category:History of Oita, Oita]] [[Category:Kofun of Oita prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] [[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Oita|WD=Q199609|1=Ōita (city)}}|coordinates={{Coord|33.216055555555556|N|131.63380555555557|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Japanese Kofun |name=Takiō Hyakketsu Yokoanabo Cluster |alternate_name=Takiō Hyakketsu |image=[[File:Takiō Hyakketsu 01.jpg|300px]] |image_caption=Takiō Hyakketsu Yokoanabo Cluster |location={{ill|Ōita (city)|en|Ōita (city)|WD=Q199609|lt=Oita City|1=Ōita (city)}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Oita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}}, Haneda |coordinates={{Coord|33|12|57.8|N|131|38|01.7|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title}} |built=6th century (Late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}) |historic_site_Historic Site=Designated as a city historic site on January 9, 1974 (Shōwa 49){{Cite web|language=ja|author=Oita City Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/culturalproperty.html|title=Oita City's Historic site|publisher=Oita City|date=2021-08-05|accessdate=2021-08-11}} |map_size=75 cave tombs |image=滝尾百穴01.jpg |map={{location map|Japan Oita|map_dot_label=Takiō Hyakketsu
Yokoanabo Cluster|mark=Yokoanabo.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Oita Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Takiō Hyakketsu Yokoanabo Cluster''' (滝尾百穴横穴古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|cave tombs|lt=Yokoanabos|ja|横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cave tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}} dating to the late 6th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, located in {{ill|Ōita (city)|en|Ōita (city)|WD=Q199609|lt=Oita City|1=Ōita (city)}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Oita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}}, Haneda. It is also referred to simply as '''Takiō Hyakketsu'''. The site has been Historic site as a {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} by Oita City. == Overview == The tombs are carved into a volcanic ash cliff north of the sports ground at {{ill|Oita City Takiō Junior High School|ja|大分市立滝尾中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oita City Takiō Junior High School|1=Q11432734|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432734}}. A total of 75 tombs of various sizes are arranged in three to four tiers. The lids of the cave tombs have collapsed and no longer remain. The tombs exhibit a variety of types, with square and rectangular floor plans, and ceilings shaped in dome, semi-cylindrical, and gabled styles such as {{ill|Hip roof|en|Hip roof|WD=Q162034|lt=Yosemune-zukuri|1=Hip roof}}. Some tombs feature coffin plattypes on the floor, while others have stone slabs (sode-ishi) positioned on one side of the entrance passage ({{ill|genkan|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/genkan|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}).[http://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o204/bunkasports/shitebunkazai/1094724531265.html Exploring Oita City's History 3 (Historic site of the Central Region)] Oita City[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Yokoanabo Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita)[https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Yokoanabo Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association This cave tomb cluster is believed to have been used as a collective family burial site. == Access == * '''By Bus''' – Take the {{ill|Oita Bus|ja|大分バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oita Bus|1=Oita Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432629}} bound for Hagiwara, {{ill|Akros|ja|あけのアクロスタウン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akros|1=AKENO ACROSS TOWN|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257072}}, {{ill|Park Place Oita|ja|パークプレイス大分|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Park Place Oita|1=Park Place Oita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11329797}}, Park City Akino, or Minami-Kuge, and alight at the Mera Bypass Entrance stop. Walk approximately 10 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5485 Takiō Hyakketsu Yokoanabo Cluster | Spot Intypeation] Official Oita Prefecture Tourism Website (Tourism Oita) * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/10 Takiō Hyakketsu Yokoanabo Cluster | Tourist Spot] Oita City Tourism Association == Distribution and Notable Yokoanabo Toional Historic site, 161 tombs == * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoanabo Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takio Hyakka Yokoanabo Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita, Ōita|qq=}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki, Miyazaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}}) - National Historic site == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013).
